faq.hotkeyCustomizationAnswer: $đź—ˇYes, we support customized hotkeys, but we do not currently have a hotkey editor. If you want to use customized hotkeys, launch StarCraft: Remastered through the Blizzard launcher and customize them there first. After doing so, future launches through ShieldBattery will use those hotkeys.đź—ˇ!
faq.isShieldBatteryFreeQuestion: $đź—ˇDoes ShieldBattery cost anything? How can I support the project?đź—ˇ!
faq.linuxSupportAnswer: $đź—ˇ<0>This is not currently something that we have official support for.</0><1>However, there's a community-contributed integration of ShieldBattery with Wine. Check out our <2>Discord</2> to learn how to run ShieldBattery on Wine.</1>đź—ˇ!
faq.linuxSupportQuestion: $đź—ˇDoes ShieldBattery work on Linux or MacOS?đź—ˇ!
faq.officialSupportAnswer: $đź—ˇNo, ShieldBattery is a project developed by passionate community members, and has no official support or acknowledgement from Blizzard.đź—ˇ!
faq.officialSupportQuestion: $đź—ˇIs ShieldBattery developed with the support of Blizzard?đź—ˇ!
faq.openSourceAnswer: $đź—ˇ<0>Yes. You can access our main repository as well as the various additional projects we've written and separated into their own repositories at our <2>GitHub page</2>.</0><1>We could always use more contributors, so if you think you can help, check it out!</1>đź—ˇ!
faq.reportIssuesAnswer: $đź—ˇThe easiest way to report bugs or issues is through our <2>Discord</2>. If you are comfortable with doing so, you can also file issues on our <5>GitHub</5>.đź—ˇ!
faq.reportIssuesQuestion: $đź—ˇHow can I report bugs or issues?đź—ˇ!
faq.supportedStarcraftVersionAnswer: $đź—ˇShieldBattery supports the latest version of StarCraft: Remastered. You can download the free version of StarCraft: Remastered from the <2>Battle.net launcher</2>. Any purchased addons (such as HD graphics) will be usable on ShieldBattery.đź—ˇ!
faq.supportedStarcraftVersionQuestion: $đź—ˇWhat version of StarCraft does ShieldBattery support?đź—ˇ!
faq.systemRequirementsQuestion: $đź—ˇWhat are the system requirements to play on ShieldBattery?đź—ˇ!
faq.title: $đź—ˇFrequently Asked Questionsđź—ˇ!
faq.systemRequirementsAnswer: $đź—ˇ<0>Our system requirements are mainly driven by those of <2>StarCraft: Remastered</2>, but in brief:</0><1><0>A computer running Windows 10 or later</0><1>2GB RAM</1><2>NVIDIA GeForce 6800 (256MB) or ATI Radeon X1600 Pro (256MB) or better</2><3>A StarCraft: Remastered installation, patched to the latest version</3></1>đź—ˇ!
faq.isShieldBatteryFreeAnswer: $đź—ˇ<0>ShieldBattery is totally free to use!</0><1>It does, however, cost us time and money to host and develop ShieldBattery. If you would like to help us cover those costs, we'd greatly appreciate it. We have set up a number of ways to contribute:</1>đź—ˇ!

splash.blurb: $đź—ˇShieldBattery is a community server that supports official StarCraft: Remastered clients. Download our launcher to get access to 1v1 and team matchmaking, improved netcode, fast replay launching, and more!đź—ˇ!
splash.disclaimerText: $đź—ˇStarCraft is a registered trademark of Blizzard Entertainment, Inc. ShieldBattery is developed solely by members of the community, unaffiliated with Blizzard, and is not officially endorsed or supported by Blizzard.đź—ˇ!
splash.subtitle: $đź—ˇThe premier StarCraft 1 community platformđź—ˇ!

shieldbatteryHealth.middleContents: $đź—ˇThis is often the result of installed anti-virus software taking action on false positives. You may need to add exceptions for these files, or tell the software to remove them from quarantine. You can also try re-installing ShieldBattery.đź—ˇ!

starcraftHealth.installPathContents: $đź—ˇYour StarCraft path setting does not point to a valid installation. Please correct the value in <2>Settings</2>. If you do not have the game installed, it can be easily downloaded from <5><0>Battle.net</0></5>. You may need to restart ShieldBattery after installation.đź—ˇ!
